name: transitland-vscode-extension
description: GTFS feed tools, DMFR editing, and Transitland integration for VS Code.

transitland.apiKey.description: Transitland API key for feed status (CodeLens). Leave empty to use TRANSITLAND_API_KEY env var.

transitland.cliPath.description: Path to the transitland CLI binary. Leave empty to auto-detect from PATH (Homebrew, go install, etc.).

commands.title: Apply opinionated format to current DMFR file

chatParticipants.name: transitland
chatParticipants.description: DMFR editing and GTFS feed tools for the Transitland Atlas

commands.name: inspect
commands.description: Inspect a GTFS feed's agencies, routes and stops

languageModelTools.name: transitland_validate
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validate a GTFS zip file or URL using the transitland CLI. Runs best-practices checks and returns a pass/fail summary with SHA1, calendar date range, and full error/warning details.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Validate a GTFS feed for spec compliance errors and warnings

feed.description: URL or local file path of a GTFS zip to validate

rtUrls.description: GTFS-RT feed URLs to pair with the static feed during validation

errorLimit.description: Max detailed errors per error group (default: 1000)

languageModelTools.name: transitland_inspect
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspect a GTFS zip file or URL. Returns agencies, routes, stops, file list, feed info, and optional service level calendar data.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Inspect GTFS feed entities and structure

feed.description: URL or local file path of a GTFS zip to inspect

languageModelTools.name: transitland_inspect_rt
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspect a GTFS Realtime protobuf feed URL. Returns vehicle positions, trip updates, and service alerts with entity counts and details.

url.description: URL or local file path of a GTFS Realtime protobuf feed to inspect

languageModelTools.name: transitland_feed_info
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Look up a feed in the Transitland archive by its onestop_id. Returns feed metadata including the latest version SHA1, calendar date range, URLs, and license info. Requires a Transitland API key configured in settings.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Look up a feed in the Transitland archive

languageModelTools.name: transitland_dmfr_schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the full DMFR JSON schema describing valid fields, types, and enum values for feed records and operator records in .dmfr.json files. Call this when you need to know what fields are allowed, required, or what values are valid.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Look up the DMFR JSON schema

languageModelTools.name: transitland_read_dmfr
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read a .dmfr.json file and return all its feeds (with operator names and all URL fields) and top-level operators as structured text. If no file path is given, reads the currently active editor. ALWAYS use this tool instead of running Python, shell commands, or scripts to inspect DMFR file contents.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read a DMFR file's feeds and operators

file.description: Path to the .dmfr.json file relative to the workspace root. Omit to use the active editor.

languageModelTools.name: transitland_search_feeds
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search for feeds across all .dmfr.json files in the workspace. Matches the query string against feed IDs, URLs, or spec types. Use this to find which file contains a particular feed, URL pattern, or operator. Returns matching feeds with their source file paths.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search feeds across workspace DMFR files

query.description: Search string to match against feeds

field.description: Which field to search in (default: any)

languageModelTools.name: transitland_gtfs_spec
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the GTFS Schedule or GTFS Realtime specification as markdown. Use the 'file' parameter to retrieve only the section for a specific GTFS file (e.g. 'stops.txt', 'routes.txt', 'stop_times.txt'). Use type='rt' for the GTFS Realtime spec. Call this when you need to understand GTFS field definitions, presence requirements, or data format rules.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Look up the GTFS or GTFS-RT specification

file.description: Optional GTFS filename to filter to (e.g. 'stops.txt')

languageModelTools.name: transitland_compare_feed_sources
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Compare two or more alternative GTFS data sources for the same service or agency — for example, a direct agency URL vs. a third-party aggregator URL. Downloads and inspects each feed to compare SHA1, calendar coverage, agencies, routes, stops, trips, feed_info, and validation issues. Returns a verdict recommending which source is more complete, recent, or active. NOTE: this tool is for comparing competing sources of the same service, not for diffing two historical versions of the same feed. Use this when a DMFR feed record lists multiple candidate URLs and you need to decide which to use as static_current.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compare alternative GTFS data sources for the same agency or service

urls.description: Two or more GTFS feed URLs or local file paths representing alternative data sources for the same service or agency

apiKey.description: Transitland API key for archive lookup (optional; falls back to TRANSITLAND_API_KEY env var)

languageModelTools.name: transitland_ntd_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the US National Transit Database (NTD) GTFS Weblinks dataset (data.transportation.gov). Returns matching agencies with their NTD ID (for use as operator tags.us_ntd_id in DMFR), city, state, transit modes, and GTFS feed URLs (candidate static_current values). Query by agency name (partial match) or numeric NTD ID; optionally filter by two-letter US state code. Use this when adding or updating a US transit operator in the Atlas to find the correct us_ntd_id tag and to discover candidate GTFS URLs.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search US NTD agencies for NTD IDs and GTFS feed URLs

query.description: Agency name to search (partial match), or a numeric NTD ID (1–5 digits, zero-padding optional)

state.description: Two-letter US state code to narrow results (e.g. CA, WA, TX)

languageModelTools.name: transitland_agencies_in_feed
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all agencies defined in a GTFS feed (agency.txt), along with a brief feed summary (SHA1, calendar range, route/stop/trip counts). Use this when setting up operator records or associated_feeds crosslinks in DMFR — it gives you the official agency names, agency_ids, websites, and timezones without the large routes/stops output that transitland_inspect produces. Prefer this over transitland_inspect when you only need to know which agencies are represented in a feed.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List agencies in a GTFS feed for operator/associated_feeds setup

feed.description: URL or local file path of a GTFS zip

languageModelTools.name: transitland_set_field
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set or remove a single field in a feed or operator record inside a .dmfr.json file. Locate the record by type ('feed' or 'operator') and its ID, then specify the field using dot-notation (e.g. 'tags.us_ntd_id', 'urls.static_current', 'license.spdx_identifier', 'name'). Pass value=null to remove the field. Intermediate objects are created automatically (e.g. setting 'tags.us_ntd_id' creates a 'tags' object if it doesn't exist). The file is formatted in-place after the edit using the transitland CLI.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Set or remove a field in a DMFR feed or operator record

file.description: Workspace-relative path to the .dmfr.json file. Omit to use the active editor.

recordType.description: Type of record to update: 'feed' (feeds[] entry matched by id) or 'operator' (top-level operators[] or feed-nested operators[] entry)

id.description: Feed onestop_id (e.g. f-9q9-caltrain) or operator onestop_id / operator_onestop_id

value.description: New string value, or null to remove the field

languageModelTools.name: transitland_add_feed
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Add a new feed record to a .dmfr.json file. Provide a feedId (f-<geohash>-<name> format — use f-FIXME-<slug> if the geohash is unknown), one or more URLs, and optional metadata. Spec is auto-detected: staticUrl only → gtfs; any realtime URL only → gtfs-rt; both → gtfs (realtime should be a separate gtfs-rt record). Scans all workspace .dmfr.json files for duplicate URLs and fails if any are found — use the existing record instead. Optionally set license (spdxIdentifier, useWithoutAttribution, etc.) and authorization (type, paramName, infoUrl) at creation time; these can also be set later with transitland_set_field. Use name only for large aggregated multi-agency feeds (e.g. UK BODS); omit for ordinary single-agency feeds.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Add a new feed record to a .dmfr.json file

file.description: Workspace-relative path to the .dmfr.json file. Omit to use the active editor.

feedId.description: Feed onestop_id (e.g. f-9q9-caltrain). Use f-FIXME-<slug> as a placeholder if the geohash is unknown.

spec.description: Feed spec. Auto-detected from provided URLs if omitted.

name.description: Human-readable feed name. Use only for large aggregated feeds covering many agencies. Omit for ordinary single-agency feeds.

spdxIdentifier.description: SPDX license code, e.g. CC-BY-4.0, ODbL-1.0, CC0-1.0

authorization.description: Authorization required to download this feed (all fields optional)

infoUrl.description: URL where users can obtain credentials

languageModelTools.name: transitland_add_operator
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Add an operator record to a .dmfr.json file. Two placements: 'nested' embeds the operator inside an existing static feed's operators[] array (the feed link is implicit — do not repeat it in associatedFeeds); 'top_level' adds the operator to the root operators[] array and requires at least one associatedFeeds entry with a feedOnestopId pointing to the static feed. Rules for associatedFeeds: single-agency nested → omit associatedFeeds entirely; multi-agency nested → one entry with gtfsAgencyId only; nested + RT → one entry per RT feed with feedOnestopId; top_level → one entry per linked feed (static + RT), each with feedOnestopId, plus gtfsAgencyId on the static feed entry if multi-agency. An operator can never be nested under a gtfs-rt feed — link RT feeds via associatedFeeds feedOnestopId instead.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Add an operator record to a .dmfr.json file

file.description: Workspace-relative path to the .dmfr.json file. Omit to use the active editor.

placement.description: Where to place the operator: 'nested' inside a static feed's operators[], or 'top_level' in the root operators[].

feedId.description: Required when placement is 'nested'. The onestop_id of the static feed to nest under.

shortName.description: Common short name or brand name.

associatedFeeds.description: Feed linkages. Omit for single-agency nested operators. See modelDescription for rules.

feedOnestopId.description: feed_onestop_id to link (RT feeds, external static feeds, or the static feed for top_level operators).

gtfsAgencyId.description: gtfs_agency_id from agency.txt. Only needed for multi-agency feeds.

languageModelTools.name: transitland_dmfr_format
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run the transitland DMFR linter/formatter on a .dmfr.json file, normalizing field order, whitespace, and feed/operator ordering in-place. The editing tools (transitland_set_field, transitland_add_feed, transitland_add_operator) call this automatically after each write, but you can call it explicitly if the CLI was unavailable during a previous edit or after any manual file change.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lint and format a .dmfr.json file in-place

file.description: Workspace-relative path to the .dmfr.json file. Omit to use the active editor.

scripts.pretest: yarn run build && yarn run lint
